chapter_context.summary: Presents the beautiful Messianic prophecy of the Rod from the stem of Jesse, describing his righteous reign, the peaceful kingdom of nature, and the gathering of the remnant.

outline.text: The Branch from the Stem of Jesse and His Righteous Reign
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Branch from the Stem of Jesse and His Righteous Reign (11:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: The Peaceful Harmony of the Renewed Creation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Peaceful Harmony of the Renewed Creation (11:6-9), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: The Gathering of the Remnant of Israel and Judah
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Gathering of the Remnant of Israel and Judah (11:10-16), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 11, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
